questions: What are the Terms of Service and when were they last updated?
answer: These Terms of Service (the ‘Agreement’) govern your (‘Client’) purchase and use of the advertising and marketing services provided by Lofi Marketing LLC (‘Company,’ ‘we,’ ‘us’) via meetlofi.com and related applications (‘Services’). By clicking ‘I ACCEPT,’ placing an order, or using the Services, you agree to be bound by this Agreement, including any Order Form and the Privacy Policy. Last Updated: June 23, 2025.

questions: How do the Terms define key concepts (Ad Content, Client Data, Order Form, Third-Party Services)?
answer: Ad Content: any text, images, videos, or other materials Client provides or approves for campaigns. Client Data: all data (including personal data) that Client uploads, submits, or collects via the Services. Order Form: the ordering document (online or written) describing Services, fees, Service Term, deliverables, and special terms. Third-Party Services: platforms/tools provided by Meta, Google, TikTok, etc., used by Company to run campaigns on Client’s behalf.

questions: What Services and support does Lofi provide?
answer: Scope: Company will provide the Services described in the Order Form using commercially reasonable efforts. Campaign Management: Company will create, launch, monitor, and optimize advertising campaigns on Third-Party Services consistent with Client budgets and goals. Support: technical and account support is provided as described in our standard support policy. AI-Generated Output: certain Services leverage AI models to generate copy, images, or recommendations (‘Output’). Client retains rights in inputs and output but is solely responsible for vetting output for accuracy, non-infringement, and compliance; Company disclaims liability for errors, biases, or regulatory issues from AI-generated materials or Third-Party platform policies. Platform Access: functionality depends on continued access to Third-Party APIs/integrations; limitations, deprecations, or suspensions may impact features; Company is not liable for such changes but will use commercially reasonable efforts to maintain functionality and provide notice. Beta Features: experimental features are provided ‘as-is’ and may change or be discontinued without notice.

questions: What are my obligations as a Client?
answer: Approvals & Budgets: Client approves Ad Content, sets budgets, and authorizes spend. Compliance: Client is solely responsible for ensuring Ad Content and settings comply with Third-Party platform policies and applicable laws (FTC, CAN-SPAM, CCPA/CPRA, GDPR, and other consumer-protection, privacy, or competition rules). Company is not responsible and has no liability for fines, sanctions, suspensions, bans, or other consequences from non-compliance. Prohibited Content/Industries: Client may not direct Company to create or run campaigns for content/industries that violate platform policies or law (e.g., adult, tobacco, weapons, unapproved pharmaceuticals, get-rich-quick, hate speech, deceptive practices). Company may refuse/suspend Services if content/industry is prohibited. Account Access & Security: Client must grant necessary access and secure its hardware, software, credentials, and Client Data. Results Not Guaranteed: performance may vary due to factors outside Company’s control (market demand, competition, audience behavior, platform algorithms, creative effectiveness).

questions: How do fees, invoicing, and taxes work?
answer: Fees: Client will pay the fees in the Order Form; Third-Party ad spend is billed separately by each platform. Invoicing & Payment: invoices are due net 30; late payments accrue 1.5% per month or the maximum permitted by law. Taxes: Client is responsible for applicable taxes, duties, and surcharges (excluding Company’s income taxes). Disputes: billing disputes must be raised within 60 days of the invoice date. Subscription & Usage: the subscription fee covers access to the Lofi platform and core Services; ad spend and any performance-based fees (e.g., % of ad spend or revenue) are billed separately and outlined in the Order Form.

questions: What are the term, termination rights, and suspension conditions?
answer: Term: the Initial Service Term is set in the Order Form and renews automatically for the same duration unless either party provides 30 days’ prior written notice. Termination for Cause: either party may terminate on 30 days’ notice for uncured material breach; Company may terminate immediately for non-payment. Effect: accrued fees through termination remain due; confidentiality, IP, indemnity, limitation of liability, and governing law survive. Suspension: Company may suspend access if Client breaches the Agreement, fails to pay fees, maintains non-compliant content, or harms the platform, reputation, or other customers.

questions: Who owns what intellectual property?
answer: Client Ownership: Client retains all rights in Ad Content and Client Data. License: Client grants Company a limited, non-exclusive license to use Ad Content and Client Data solely to perform the Services. Company Materials: Company retains rights in its proprietary methodologies, systems, software, and materials used to deliver the Services.

questions: How is confidential information handled and how may data be used?
answer: Each party will protect the other’s Confidential Information with at least the same care it uses for its own. ‘Confidential Information’ excludes information that is publicly available, known prior to disclosure, rightfully obtained from a third party, or independently developed. Data Use for Optimization: Client agrees that anonymized, aggregate data may be used by Company to improve algorithms, targeting performance, and product features; Company will not share identifiable Client Data with third parties without consent.

questions: What are the privacy and data-protection commitments?
answer: Company’s Privacy Policy governs collection and use of personal data. Under applicable data-protection laws (e.g., GDPR, CCPA/CPRA), Company acts as a processor of Client Data. Both parties will comply with their respective legal obligations, including honoring data subject rights and maintaining appropriate safeguards.

questions: What warranties apply and what is disclaimed?
answer: Limited Warranty: Company warrants Services will be performed in a professional manner consistent with industry standards. Disclaimer: EXCEPT AS STATED, SERVICES ARE PROVIDED ‘AS IS,’ AND COMPANY DISCLAIMS ALL OTHER WARRANTIES, EXPRESS OR IMPLIED, INCLUDING MERCHANTABILITY AND FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE.

questions: What is the limitation of liability?
answer: To the maximum extent permitted by law, Company’s aggregate liability arising out of or related to the Agreement shall not exceed the fees paid by Client in the 12 months preceding the claim. Company is not liable for indirect, special, incidental, or consequential damages.

questions: Who indemnifies whom and for what?
answer: By Client: Client will indemnify, defend, and hold Company harmless from claims arising from Client’s Ad Content, breach of the Agreement, or failure to comply with platform policies or laws. By Company: Company will indemnify Client for third-party claims that Company’s proprietary Services infringe U.S. patents or copyrights, subject to Company’s right to replace or modify the infringing element.

questions: How are force majeure events and third-party platform changes handled?
answer: Neither party is liable for delays or failures due to events beyond reasonable control, including acts of God, strikes, Internet outages, or Third-Party API deprecations or policy changes. Company will use reasonable efforts to adapt, but is not responsible for campaign disruptions or increased spend caused thereby.

questions: What law governs and where are disputes resolved?
answer: The Agreement is governed by the laws of the State of California, without regard to conflict-of-law rules. Disputes are resolved exclusively in the state or federal courts located in San Francisco County, California.

questions: What miscellaneous provisions apply (assignment, notices, publicity, entire agreement, subcontractors)?
answer: Assignment: Client may not assign without Company’s prior written consent; Company may assign to an affiliate or successor. Notices: must be in writing and sent to the addresses in the Order Form. Publicity: Client grants Company a non-exclusive, worldwide, royalty-free license to use Client’s name, logo, and anonymized performance metrics in marketing materials, case studies, and press releases, unless Client objects in writing. Entire Agreement/Amendments: this Agreement, the Order Form, and the Privacy Policy constitute the entire agreement; amendments must be in writing signed by both parties. Subcontractors: Company may use subcontractors/independent contractors but remains responsible for their work and compliance.

questions: Are there AI-related disclosures I must consider?
answer: Certain outputs may be generated using AI or machine-learning systems. Client is responsible for disclosing the use of AI-generated content if required by platform policies or industry-specific advertising regulations.

questions: How do I contact Lofi about legal questions?
answer: Lofi Marketing LLC. Email: legal@meetlofi.com.

questions: What does Lofi’s Privacy Policy cover and when is it effective?
answer: Lofi Marketing LLC (‘Lofi,’ ‘we,’ ‘our,’ ‘us’) values the privacy of individuals who visit our websites, use our applications, and interact with related services (collectively, the ‘Services’). This Privacy Policy explains how we collect, use, disclose, and protect information about users of the Services (‘you,’ ‘Users’). Your use of the Services is also subject to our Terms of Service. Effective Date: March 1, 2025. B2B focus & roles: When we process data about visitors, leads, and our own accounts, Lofi acts as a data controller. When we process personal data that business clients provide for advertising (e.g., audience lists, ad account data), we act as a data processor under a Data Processing Addendum (DPA). Clients are responsible for obtaining necessary notices, permissions, and lawful bases to provide such data to Lofi.

questions: What is the scope of this Privacy Policy?
answer: This Policy applies to information collected through our websites (including meetlofi.com), web/mobile apps, and any products or services that link to it. It does not apply to data-handling practices of third-party sites or applications you access via links from the Services. Those third parties’ policies govern their practices.

questions: What information does Lofi collect?
answer: A) Information you provide: Account & profile (name, email, company, role, password), communications (emails, chats, support requests, surveys), marketing preferences (subscriptions), and payment information (processed by our payment processor, e.g., Stripe; we do not store full card details). B) Automatically collected: Device & log data (IP, device IDs, browser, OS, language, referrers, pages, timestamps), usage data (feature usage, clicks, time on page, diagnostics), and cookies/pixels/local storage/beacons for operations, analytics, and advertising (see Cookies & Ads). C) From third parties: Ad platforms and partners (campaign and conversion metrics), service providers (support, CRM, analytics, cloud), and social/lead sources (public or shared business contact info) in compliance with law.

questions: How does Lofi use collected information?
answer: We use information to: provide and operate the Services (accounts, security, support); run and optimize marketing campaigns on your behalf as processor according to your instructions; improve the Services and develop features (troubleshooting, analytics, research); personalize experiences; communicate about updates, security alerts, and administrative or marketing messages (you can opt out of marketing); process payments; prevent fraud, abuse, and security incidents; and comply with legal obligations and enforce our Terms. AI tools: some features use AI models to assist with creative generation or recommendations. We configure systems and vendors so prompts/outputs and related data are processed only to provide the Services and not used to train generalized models without appropriate instructions or agreements. You remain responsible for reviewing AI-assisted content for accuracy and compliance.

questions: How does Lofi handle client-provided campaign data as a processor?
answer: When clients upload or grant access to personal data for advertising (e.g., hashed emails for audience matching, creative assets, performance metrics), Lofi processes such data solely on the client’s documented instructions and the DPA. Clients must ensure a valid lawful basis (e.g., consent/opt-in, legitimate interests) and required notices for ad-tech uses; are responsible for platform/regulatory compliance (e.g., Meta/Google policies, privacy and advertising laws); and must not provide personal data that violates law or platform rules.

questions: What legal bases does Lofi rely on (EEA/UK)?
answer: Where GDPR/UK GDPR applies, we process personal data on: Contract (Art. 6(1)(b)) to provide requested Services; Legitimate interests (Art. 6(1)(f)) such as securing and improving the Services and B2B marketing (balanced with your rights); Consent (Art. 6(1)(a)) for certain cookies/advertising and email marketing where required; Legal obligation (Art. 6(1)(c)) for compliance and recordkeeping.

questions: With whom does Lofi share information?
answer: We may share with: service providers/processors (cloud hosting, analytics, security, support, CRM, payments, A/B testing, AI infrastructure) bound by confidentiality and data-processing terms; ad platforms to run and measure campaigns you authorize; professional advisors and authorities (to comply with law, enforce terms, protect rights and safety); affiliates for Services consistent with this Policy; and in business transfers (mergers, acquisitions, financing, asset sales) subject to this Policy. We do not sell personal information for money. For California CPRA, certain advertising cookies/pixels may be considered ‘sharing’ for cross-context behavioral advertising; see Your Rights for opt-outs.

questions: How long does Lofi retain information?
answer: We retain information as needed to provide the Services, comply with legal obligations, resolve disputes, and enforce agreements. Typical periods: account data for the account lifetime and a reasonable period thereafter (e.g., 12–24 months) unless deletion is requested or required sooner; campaign data (processor role) for the term in the DPA or as directed by the client, then deleted or returned; logs/analytics for limited periods (e.g., 12–18 months), then aggregated or deleted.

questions: How does Lofi use cookies, analytics, and advertising technologies?
answer: Types: strictly necessary cookies for core functions (authentication, security); analytics (page performance, usage patterns); and advertising to measure conversions and deliver/limit ads. Your choices: use browser settings to block/delete cookies (some features may not function); where required, use our consent banner to accept, reject, or manage non-essential cookies; manage platform ad settings (Google Ad Settings, Meta Ad Preferences, LinkedIn Ads). Do Not Track: our Services do not respond to DNT signals. We honor Global Privacy Control (GPC) signals for CPRA ‘sale/share’ opt-outs.

questions: What privacy rights do I have under California and certain U.S. state laws?
answer: Under CPRA (and, as applicable, CO/CT/VA/UT, etc.), you may have rights to know/access categories and specific pieces of personal information collected; delete personal information; correct inaccurate personal information; opt out of ‘sale’ or ‘sharing’ for cross-context behavioral advertising (we do not sell for money, but may ‘share’ via ads/analytics pixels); limit use/disclosure of sensitive personal information (we do not use SPI for inferring characteristics); and be free from discrimination for exercising rights. Exercise rights by emailing privacy@meetlofi.com or using our web form (if available). We will verify requests and respond as required by law. Opt out of sale/share via the footer link ‘Do Not Sell or Share My Personal Information’ and/or by sending a GPC signal, which we honor.

questions: What rights do I have under GDPR/UK GDPR?
answer: You may have the right to access, rectify, erase, restrict or object to processing, and data portability. Where we rely on consent, you may withdraw it at any time. You also have the right to lodge a complaint with your local supervisory authority. We will honor valid requests in accordance with applicable law.

questions: How does Lofi handle international data transfers?
answer: We are headquartered in the United States and may transfer personal data to countries that may not provide the same level of protection as your jurisdiction. Where required, we use appropriate safeguards—such as Standard Contractual Clauses and additional measures. Contact us for details.

questions: How does Lofi protect personal information?
answer: We implement administrative, technical, and physical safeguards designed to protect personal information (e.g., access controls, encryption in transit, vulnerability management). No method of transmission or storage is 100% secure; we cannot guarantee absolute security.

questions: Does Lofi collect information from children?
answer: The Services are not directed to children and we do not knowingly collect personal information from children under 13 (or under 16 where a higher age threshold applies). If you believe a child has provided personal information to us, contact privacy@meetlofi.com and we will take appropriate action.

questions: What choices do I have about my information and communications?
answer: Access & updates: review and update certain account information in your settings or by contacting us. Marketing communications: opt out of promotional emails using the unsubscribe link or by contacting us. We may still send transactional or service messages necessary to operate your account.

questions: What is Lofi’s California Notice at Collection?
answer: We collect categories of personal information such as identifiers (name, email, IP, device IDs), customer records (billing contact, limited payment metadata—full card details via processor), commercial information (subscription level, transaction history), Internet/network activity (pages viewed, referrers, timestamps), general geolocation (approximate from IP), professional/employment information (company, role), and inferences (preferences from usage). Sources include you, automatic collection, and partners. Purposes include providing and improving the Services, security, analytics, marketing, and advertising measurement. Disclosures are to service providers, payment processors, analytics/ad partners, and affiliates as described. Sensitive PI: we do not collect SPI for inferring characteristics. Sale/Share: we do not sell personal information for money; certain identifiers/network data/inferences may be ‘shared’ for cross-context advertising. Opt out via the footer link and by using GPC, which we honor. Retention periods are described in our retention section.

questions: How does Lofi handle third-party links?
answer: Our Services may contain links to third-party sites or services. We are not responsible for their privacy practices. Review their policies before providing information.

questions: How will I know if Lofi updates this Privacy Policy?
answer: We may update this Policy from time to time. We will post the updated version and revise the ‘Effective Date’ above. Material changes will be communicated via the Services or by email where appropriate.

questions: How can I contact Lofi about privacy?
answer: Lofi Marketing LLC, Attn: Privacy. Email: legal@meetlofi.com. For EEA/UK users: if applicable, contact our EU/UK representative at the address we provide upon request. For California residents: use the ‘Do Not Sell or Share My Personal Information’ link in our footer to exercise CPRA opt-out rights.
